message:
test(conformance): execute socket_ops fixtures in-process + via harness (bd-xejg, bd-ho3il)

Adds two test functions to
crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/socket_ops_conformance_test.rs:

1. `socket_ops_fixture_cases_match_execute_fixture_case` — dispatches
   every fixture case through `execute_fixture_case` and asserts
   impl_output == expected_output plus host_parity.
2. `socket_ops_fixture_executes_with_host_parity_via_harness_matrix` —
   re-runs every case through the `harness conformance-matrix-case`
   subprocess (the CI conformance path), catching packaging /
   dispatch regressions that the in-process path would miss.

Before this change, socket_ops_conformance_test.rs only validated
fixture metadata (schema, coverage names, error codes, mode
distribution); no case ever actually executed through the shared
executor path even though the underlying dispatches
(execute_socket_case / execute_bind_case / execute_listen_case /
execute_shutdown_case / execute_getsockname_case at
crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:11109-11175) were already
wired.

No parity regressions exposed.

Validation: cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test
socket_ops_conformance_test -> 14 passed, 0 failed (0.14s) via rch
on vmi1149989.

Also marks bd-ho3il closed: commit 9ea2ad56 already broadened the
fprintf executor matrix to match snprintf arg-shape parity, the
bead was just never flipped to closed. Verified both run_impl_fprintf
(lib.rs:2892-2948) and run_host_fprintf (lib.rs:2954-3011) now have
identical arm sets to the snprintf counterparts.

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -577,7 +577,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-am9u.3","title":"Increase CPU affinity mask from 128 bytes (1024 CPUs) to support larger systems","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:1804
CURRENT: let mut mask = [0u8; 128] — supports max 1024 CPUs.
PROBLEM: Large servers can have >1024 logical CPUs (e.g., AMD EPYC with 256 cores + SMT = 512, multi-socket = 1024+). The mask should be sized to the actual system or use a reasonable upper bound.
FIX: Either use a larger static buffer (e.g., 512 bytes = 4096 CPUs) or dynamically query the kernel for the actual affinity mask size. The sched_getaffinity syscall returns the actual size used, so we could do a two-pass approach.
IMPACT: Low — only affects very large servers. Current 1024 CPU limit is sufficient for most systems.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:53.388076511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:14:19.511676710Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:14:19.510452740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.3","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:53.388076511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-am9u.4","title":"Make default thread stack size configurable via environment variable","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2488,2494
CURRENT: ATTR_DEFAULT_STACK_SIZE = 2 * 1024 * 1024 (2 MiB), ATTR_MIN_STACK_SIZE = 16384 (16 KiB)
CONTEXT: glibc uses a default of 8 MiB, musl uses 128 KiB. Our 2 MiB is a reasonable middle ground but differs from both.
FIX: Keep 2 MiB as default but allow override via FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_STACK_SIZE env var. The minimum should match PTHREAD_STACK_MIN from the system.
IMPACT: Low — 2 MiB works for most applications. Some embedded or memory-constrained scenarios might want smaller stacks.
CONSIDERATION: Don't read env var on every thread creation — cache it at init time.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:15:51.871640506Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:15:51.870681077Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.4","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-am9u.5","title":"Fix _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX from 32 to match system reality","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:1924
CURRENT: _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX = 32
PROBLEM: While glibc returns 32 for this, some systems have longer TTY names (especially for pseudo-terminals with long paths like /dev/pts/XXX). The constant should match glibc's behavior for compatibility.
FIX: Keep 32 to match glibc behavior but add a comment documenting the rationale. This is actually correct for glibc compat.
IMPACT: Very low — TTY names are almost always < 32 chars.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:05.574339644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:15.748586062Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:14.432724344Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.5","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:05.574339644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1339,"issue_id":"bd-am9u.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Verified: _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX=32 matches glibc behavior. No change needed.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:15Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-anoe7","title":"fuzz: pathname encoding edge cases (NUL, UTF-8, long, .. traversal, symlinks)","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pathname.rs targeting the pathname-parsing + canonicalization surface: realpath, canonicalize_file_name, basename/dirname, access/faccessat, stat/lstat/fstatat, readlink/readlinkat. These share a common pathname-bytes input channel and are the classic CVE surface (CVE-2018-1000001 realpath OOB, CVE-2016-1234 glibc glob).

## Archetype
1 + 3: Crash-detector on the impl + **differential** against libc for path operations that don't touch fs state (basename/dirname) and for the few that do (stat/readlink) over a pre-seeded tmpdir with a known symlink graph.

## Inputs
struct PathnameFuzzInput {
  op: u8,                 // 0=realpath, 1=basename, 2=dirname, 3=stat, 4=lstat, 5=readlink, 6=access
  bytes: Vec<u8>,         // arbitrary — rejected if it contains interior NUL (CString won't build)
  buf_size: u16,          // for readlink
  flags: i32,             // for faccessat etc.
}

## Invariants
- Never panic
- For basename/dirname: result string length <= original + some constant; bytes are a suffix/prefix of the input
- For realpath: if non-null, resolved path has no '//' or '/./' or trailing '/', all components are canonical
- For stat family: non-negative rc implies st_dev/st_ino written; errno on failure is in the documented set
- Host parity: our errno family matches libc for inert paths

## Seed corpus
- empty, '/', '.', '..', '/tmp', '/proc/self', '/dev/null', '/etc/passwd', '/////' (collapsing slashes), '../../../..', path with unicode (é, 中, emoji), path with high-bit bytes, path at NAME_MAX (255), path at PATH_MAX (4096)

## Expected bug classes
- PATH_MAX overflow, NAME_MAX overflow, NUL-embedded path, UTF-8 boundary split, symlink loop detection, '..' collapsing vs preserving, trailing-slash semantics, basename('') = '', dirname('/') = '/'

## Score
5 × 4 × /3 ≈ 6.7 — high (path CVEs are a historical goldmine).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:57.580798569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:23:54.239581894Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-anoe7","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:45.216777141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

These share a common pathname-bytes input channel and are the classic CVE surface (CVE-2018-1000001 realpath OOB, CVE-2016-1234 glibc glob).

## Archetype

5 × 4 × /3 ≈ 6.7 — high (path CVEs are a historical goldmine).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:57.580798569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:49:25.192029+00:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-anoe7","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:45.216777141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:49:25.192029+00:00","close_reason":"done","notes":"Delivered crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pathname.rs in commit 68828908. Crash-detector + invariant harness for realpath/canonicalize_file_name/basename/dirname/stat/lstat/access/readlink/fstatat/readlinkat/faccessat with 64-byte 0xFD guard sentinels around all output buffers and a PATH_MAX-bounded input cap. Host-libc differential for basename/dirname is a follow-up (both our ABI and libc export the same symbol; needs dlsym(RTLD_NEXT) plumbing). Closed via JSONL edit — br CLI lookup bug; prior close was apparently reverted by a br daemon flush of stale DB state, re-applying now."}

Agent Mail review delivery to MistyCliff attempted twice but failed due mailbox DB errors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-16T22:06:53.739313078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T22:20:52.012240065Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T22:20:52.008566060Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-ho3il","title":"[LOW] review: fprintf executor matrix lags snprintf coverage","description":"[LOW] review: fprintf executor matrix lags snprintf coverage

Recent printf pipeline changes expanded the snprintf/sprintf executor matrix, including `NullPtr` and several 3/4/5-argument combinations, but the fprintf path remains much narrower.

Current gap:
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2712-2849` handles `PrintfArg::NullPtr` and multiple 3+/4+/5-argument combinations for `run_impl_snprintf` / `run_host_snprintf`.
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2854-2910` still only supports the small fprintf subset and returns `unsupported fprintf arg combination` for anything wider.

That creates a coverage skew inside the same executor family: newly-added fixture cases can pass through snprintf/sprintf while equivalent fprintf cases are silently untestable.

Review ask:
- keep the fprintf dispatch matrix in lockstep with the snprintf/sprintf executor surface
- add at least the currently-supported `NullPtr` and 3+/4+/5-argument combinations so harness coverage does not regress by API shape","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:29:44.457254443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:32:53.511992Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","coverage","printf","review"]}
+{"id":"bd-ho3il","title":"[LOW] review: fprintf executor matrix lags snprintf coverage","description":"[LOW] review: fprintf executor matrix lags snprintf coverage

Recent printf pipeline changes expanded the snprintf/sprintf executor matrix, including `NullPtr` and several 3/4/5-argument combinations, but the fprintf path remains much narrower.

Current gap:
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2712-2849` handles `PrintfArg::NullPtr` and multiple 3+/4+/5-argument combinations for `run_impl_snprintf` / `run_host_snprintf`.
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2854-2910` still only supports the small fprintf subset and returns `unsupported fprintf arg combination` for anything wider.

That creates a coverage skew inside the same executor family: newly-added fixture cases can pass through snprintf/sprintf while equivalent fprintf cases are silently untestable.

Review ask:
- keep the fprintf dispatch matrix in lockstep with the snprintf/sprintf executor surface
- add at least the currently-supported `NullPtr` and 3+/4+/5-argument combinations so harness coverage does not regress by API shape","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:29:44.457254443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:23.967716+00:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","coverage","printf","review"],"assignee":"cc-libc","notes":"Closed by cc-libc (JSONL edit).

Verified already-landed fix in commit 9ea2ad56 ('test(conformance): broaden fprintf arg-shape coverage in run_impl + run_host'). That commit extended `run_impl_fprintf` and `run_host_fprintf` in crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs with the exact match-arms the bead asked for: [NullPtr], [Int,Int,Double], [Int,Int,Int], [Int,Int,Str], [Int,Str,Double], [Int,Int,Str,Double], and [Int,Int,Int,Int,Int].

I re-checked both dispatch matrices side by side: lines 2752-2818 (run_impl_snprintf) and lines 2892-2948 (run_impl_fprintf) — all 17 PrintfArg shape arms are now identical between the two executor paths; the only visible difference is the order in which [NullPtr] and the 2-arg shapes appear. run_host_fprintf at lines 2954-3011 mirrors run_host_snprintf at lines 2823-2885 for the same shape set, preserving the strict symmetry the bead wanted.

No additional code change needed — this closes as 'already done'.","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:23.967716+00:00","close_reason":"already done at commit 9ea2ad56"}
 {"id":"bd-hzkc6","title":"syscall: signalfd4 existing fd update with same mask should return same fd without touching errno","description":"Host glibc returns the same fd and leaves errno unchanged for signalfd(existing_fd, &mask, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the in-place update contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:56:03.717758642Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.635816783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.633658460Z","close_reason":"signalfd4(existing_fd, same_mask, 0) → same_fd (in-place update) pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-i5fto","title":"syscall: sched_get_priority_min negative policy should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for sched_get_priority_min(-1). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the negative-policy contract is explicit apart from the existing large-invalid-policy case.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:48.534935980Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:34.512419525Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:34.510363683Z","close_reason":"Added sched_get_priority_min_negative_policy_is_einval asserting EINVAL for policy=-1.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ibh7","title":"Divergent ABI source scanning between build.rs and check_replacement_guard.sh breaks standalone build","description":"`crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` scans all `.rs` files in `src/` for `libc::` callthroughs, which catches `host_resolve.rs:51 libc::dlvsym` and breaks the `standalone` build. However, `scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh` only scans `*_abi.rs` files, missing `host_resolve.rs` entirely. Furthermore, `build.rs` does not implement the newly added `source_scan_exceptions` from `replacement_profile.json`.\n\nTo fix:\n1. Make `check_replacement_guard.sh` scan all `.rs` files (or align the scopes).\n2. Update `build.rs` to respect `source_scan_exceptions` so `host_resolve::dlvsym` (and any future exceptions) can be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T19:12:58.484175575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T20:33:37.318380234Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T20:33:37.316445662Z","close_reason":"No repro; build.rs and replacement guard already align on *_abi.rs scanning and source_scan_exceptions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -973,7 +973,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-x66ds","title":"printf %g uses %e style for exponents -4..-2 (should use %f)","description":"crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs format_g() has wrong C11 threshold: 'if exp >= -(1) && exp < p as i32 {...%f...}'. C11 7.21.6.1 para 8: use %e iff exponent < -4 OR exponent >= precision. Should be 'exp >= -4 && exp < p'. Current code forces exponents -2..-4 to %e form ('1.234e-04') instead of %f ('0.0001234'). Surfaced by bd-12hh printf_conformance harness matrix (sprintf_g_basic case).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:19:39.041930823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:46:01.235915740Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:46:01.233437119Z","close_reason":"format_g threshold now correctly applies C11 7.21.6.1 para 8: use %e iff exp < -4 OR exp >= precision. sprintf_g_basic (0.0001234) now renders as 0.0001234. Committed a4789ab.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-x7jd","title":"POSIX/hosts(5) conformance table for /etc/hosts parser","description":"frankenlibc-core::resolv has unit tests for parse_hosts_line/lookup_hosts but no spec-annotated conformance table. Add Pattern-4 spec-derived table with cases citing hosts(5) + RFC 952 + RFC 1123 (hostname validation). Coverage: whitespace (spaces/tabs/mixed), case insensitivity, IPv4/IPv6 mix, multi-alias lines, comments (start/mid-line), blank lines, CRLF, malformed addresses, RFC 952/1123 hostname char set. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module. One commit per section group.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:25:33.676462834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:29:43.474768096Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:29:43.472712968Z","close_reason":"Added 3 POSIX/resolver(5)/hosts(5) spec-derived conformance tables across 2 commits: ad881c2 (hosts parse 16 cases + lookup 7 cases), 0b464ba (resolv.conf 12 cases). Total 35 cases with spec_ref citations for hosts(5), RFC 952, RFC 1123, RFC 1035, resolver(5), and <resolv.h> MAXNS cap.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-x9vt","title":"resolv: characterize NULL gaicb policy for gai_error without crashing tests","description":"Host gai_error(NULL) appears unsafe or crash-prone, while FrankenLibC currently returns a safe ENOSYS fallback. Determine the documented policy for this NULL-handle edge without destabilizing the test harness, then codify the expected behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:55.762351969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:42:44.680688756Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:42:44.678520136Z","close_reason":"Characterized host gai_error(NULL) via subprocess probe and codified FrankenLibC safe NULL-handle policy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-xejg","title":"socket: execute socket_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/socket_ops_conformance_test.rs only validates socket fixture metadata even though the shared conformance executor covers socket/bind/listen/shutdown cases. Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/socket_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity in strict/hardened modes, and fix any socket parity regressions exposed by the harness path.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:12:06.315894551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:18:12.179388627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-xejg","title":"socket: execute socket_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/socket_ops_conformance_test.rs only validates socket fixture metadata even though the shared conformance executor covers socket/bind/listen/shutdown cases. Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/socket_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity in strict/hardened modes, and fix any socket parity regressions exposed by the harness path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:12:06.315894551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:54:24.295865+00:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"assignee":"cc-libc","notes":"Closed by cc-libc. Added two test functions to crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/socket_ops_conformance_test.rs:

1. `socket_ops_fixture_cases_match_execute_fixture_case` — dispatches every fixture case through the shared `frankenlibc_fixture_exec::execute_fixture_case` entrypoint and asserts `impl_output == expected_output` + `host_parity` holds. Socket-family executors run impl-only (host_output="SKIP"); parity comes from the RawSyscall path hitting the kernel identically on both sides.
2. `socket_ops_fixture_executes_with_host_parity_via_harness_matrix` — re-runs every case through the `harness conformance-matrix-case` subprocess (same path CI uses), catching packaging / dispatch regressions in the harness binary itself, not just the in-process executor.

No socket parity regressions were exposed. Covered functions from socket_ops.json v1: socket (3 strict + 2 hardened), bind, listen, shutdown, getsockname. All dispatched through the existing execute_socket/bind/listen/shutdown/getsockname_case entrypoints at crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:11109-11175.

Validation: `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test socket_ops_conformance_test` -> 14 passed, 0 failed, 0.14s runtime via rch on vmi1149989.

Also closing bd-ho3il in the same push — already fulfilled by commit 9ea2ad56 which brought fprintf arg-shape coverage to full parity with snprintf; the bead just wasn't marked done.","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:54:24.295865+00:00","close_reason":"done"}
